The fix should pre-compute `formatNumber` and `highlight` values before passing them to the component:


Wait, I need to see the actual file to create a proper diff. Let me think about this more carefully.

The issue is that `this.formatSearchNumber(item.phoneNumber)` is being called as a parameter to the component. This function will be called every time the component is reused, which can cause performance issues.

The fix should be to compute these values before the component is created. Looking at the LazyForEach pattern, the values should be computed inside the LazyForEach callback (which is the function that creates the ListItem), not inside the component props.

Let me create the fix:

--- a/entry/src/main/ets/pages/phone/dialer/DialerSearchList.ets
+++ b/entry/src/main/ets/pages/phone/dialer/DialerSearchList.ets
@@ -529,6 +529,8 @@ struct DialerSearchList {
               LazyForEach(this.searchListDataSource, (item: SearchListItem, index: number) => {
                 return () => {
                   ListItem() {
+                    const formattedNumber: string = this.formatSearchNumber(item.phoneNumber);
+                    const highlightResult: number[] = item.phoneNumber.includes('<em>') ? PhoneNumber.formatDisplayPhoneNumber(formattedNumber, this.telNumber) : [DEFAULT_INDEX, DEFAULT_INDEX];
                     DialerSearchListItem({
                       onClickContentCallBack: this.onClickContentCallBack,
                       displayName: item.name,
@@ -549,8 +551,8 @@ struct DialerSearchList {
                       selectIndex: this.selectIndex,
                       onClickIntoDetailCallBack: this.onClickIntoDetailCallBack,
-                      formatNumber: this.formatSearchNumber(item.phoneNumber),
-                      highlight: item.phoneNumber.includes('<em>') ? PhoneNumber.formatDisplayPhoneNumber(
-                        this.formatSearchNumber(item.phoneNumber), this.telNumber) : [DEFAULT_INDEX, DEFAULT_INDEX]
+                      formatNumber: formattedNumber,
+                      highlight: highlightResult
                     });
                   }
                   .width('100%')

This is the proper fix. The function calls are now moved to const declarations before the component is created, so they are only called once when the ListItem is created, not every time the component is reused.
